pattern: A monotonic decreasing deque of indices keeps the current window's maximum at its front while evicting out-of-window and dominated elements, giving each window's max in amortized constant time.
hints: Re-scanning each window for its max is wasteful; keep candidates ordered so the maximum is always at hand.
Maintain a deque of indices whose values decrease from front to back, dropping indices that fall out of the window or are smaller than the incoming value.
Push each index after popping smaller tail values, pop the front when it leaves the window, and once the first window is full the front index's value is each window's maximum.

edge_inputs.description: single element window of size one
window equals full array, one max overall
strictly decreasing array, front always leaves
strictly increasing array, max is always the newest
negatives only, max is the least negative in window

stress.description: random values with a random valid window size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randrange(-10000, 10001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: k is always within [1, n] per constraints

stress.description: many repeated values so deque ties are exercised, with small window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randrange(-3, 4) for _ in range(n)]
    k = min(n, 3)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: narrow value range forces frequent equal-value comparisons
